sections.content: RIGHT didn't change the board either. So far UP, DOWN, LEFT, SPACE, and RIGHT have all been ineffective. Let me try MOUSE actions - maybe I need to click on specific objects. Let me also try ACTION7 which I haven't tried yet. Let me first inspect the board more carefully to understand what objects are present and where they are.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Inspect the current board state more carefully
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"Number of nodes: {len(nodes)}")
for n in nodes:
    print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")

# Also check adjacency
print(f"\nAdjacency: {seg['adjacency_list'][:10]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I can see the board structure more clearly. Let me analyze:
1. There's a large blue background (node 0)
2. A light blue vertical strip (node 1, S color)
3. Another large blue region (node 2)
4. Black vertical strip on the right edge (node 3)
5. Yellow vertical strip on the right edge (node 4)
6. A charcoal L-shape on the left (node 5)
7. A black rectangular object on the right side (node 6) with white dots inside (nodes 7-12)
8. A yellow L-shape (node 10)
9. A black horizontal strip at the bottom (node 13)
The interesting objects seem to be:
- Node 5: charcoal L-shape at the left
- Node 6: black rectangle with white dots
- Node 10: yellow L-shape
Let me try clicking on these objects with MOUSE. The white dots seem like interactive elements. Let me try clicking on one of them.
Node 7 is at [43, 55], Node 8 at [43, 58], Node 9 at [43, 61], Node 11 at [46, 61], Node 12 at [49, 61].
Let me try clicking on node 7 first.

<parameter=code>

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
